summary: Foolish Heart jam shines, solid second set despite short Space
blurb: The show delivers a soaring Foolish Heart performance and a moving Attics Of My Life, with the second set lifting the night after a modest first set. Jerry is on fire throughout, while the Drums/Space segment feels brief. Highlights also include Jack Straw and The Mighty Quinn encore.
review: Charlotte Coliseum on March 22, 1995 offered a mixed‑bag of moments that still manage to sparkle for Deadheads willing to look past the tape’s imperfections. The first set trudges along until the energy spikes with Jack Straw, but it’s the second half that truly lifts the night – a soaring Foolish Heart jam that reviewers describe as “on” despite Garcia’s health issues, followed by an emotionally resonant Attics Of My Life and a heartfelt I Want To Tell You. The Drums/Space segment is notably brief, leaving some listeners wanting more, yet the band closes strong with Turn On Your Lovelight and a joyful Mighty Quinn encore. A modest audience recording (gd95-03-22.akg.12179.sbeok.shnf) captures the warmth of the arena ambience and the key moments best, making it the go‑to source for this show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across the four recordings, three are mixed and one is positive; the consistent praise for Foolish Heart, Attics Of My Life, and the second‑set lift yields a mild positive sentiment, justifying a modest +0.4 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Warm audience tape that captures the standout Foolish Heart jam and key set moments; most detailed review and overall best balance of performance and listenability.

key_highlights: Soaring Foolish Heart jam despite Garcia’s health concerns
Moving Attics Of My Life performance in the second set
St. Stephen tease and an energetic Unchained noted by one reviewer
Short Drums/Space segment that leaves listeners wanting more

band_performance.Jerry: On fire during Foolish Heart, delivering an amazing jam even with health issues; his presence energizes the second set.
band_performance.Bob: Seen standing beside Jerry as “Bobby,” but no specific playing remarks recorded.
